column1: 3. MTA Announces Automated Camera Enforcement Expanding to Four Bronx and Brooklyn Bus Routes
# MTA Announces Automated Camera Enforcement Expanding to Four Bronx and Brooklyn Bus Routes

column3: B11 and B63 to Become ACE Equipped_

column1: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) today announced four more bus routes will now be equipped with Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE). The 60-day warning period on the Bx9
column3: B11 and B63 bus routes in the Bronx and Brooklyn will begin on Monday
column4: Nov. 10. Vehicles improperly using busways and bus lanes
column5: blocking bus stops
column6: or illegally double parked will receive warning notices in the mail for an initial period of 60 days followed by summonses thereafter. Summonses start at $50 and escalate to $250 for repeat violators.

column1: These four routes are in addition to the 47 routes currently enforced. More than 1
column2: 400 buses are now ACE equipped covering 545 miles of routes and benefiting over 840

column1: Bus routes equipped with automated enforcement on average have increased speeds by 5% with some corridors seeing gains as high as 30%. These routes have also experienced a 20% reduction in collisions; and 5% to 10% estimated reduction in emissions. There has also been a 40% reduction in bus stops being blocked by vehicles on ACE routes. Only 12% of drivers commit more than two bus lane violations after being fined.

The ACE program is administered in partnership with the New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT) and the New York City Department of Finance (NYCDOF). Once violations are captured by cameras on multiple buses
column5: location
column6: and time stamp details are then securely transmitted to NYCDOT for review by City employees and processing thereafter.

column2: ACE has rapidly expanded from a few select routes to a citywide initiative covering all five boroughs. The program has demonstrated measurable success in improving bus speeds
column5: ensuring more reliable service for the daily bus customers.

column1: Each corridor with active ACE has signage indicating that those routes are camera-enforced. Details can be found at [mta.info/ace](https://www.mta.info/ace).

column1: \*This advertisement updates the advertisement published under the same Contract number from November 6
1. The scope of this Contract will include the development and installation of software and hardware and structural modifications for ATS-B to enable deployment of the following upgrades:
a. Thin Client System for remote ATS-B access;
b. Expansion of the ATS-B Trip Editor for layered schedule editing;
c. Real-time data interface between ATS-B and the MTA Data Warehouse;
d. Enhancements to ATS-B playback tools;
e. Establishment and testing of network connections between ATS-B and the Public Address/Customer Information System;
f. ATS-B Jump Server for secure access by third parties;
g. ATS-B communication with the 5G-based DCS and carborne controllers
column2: while maintaining compatibility with existing DCS system;

column1: h. Modernization of CBTC-centric ATS-B/Programable Logic Controller interface;

column2: Zone Controller and Carborne Controller systems for cloud-based testing at MTA’s Enhanced Interoperability Test Facility (EITF);

column1: j. Replacement of end-of-useful-life ATS-B core network switches at the Operations Control Center and Backup Rail Control Center;
k. Network interfaces between ATS-B and the Crosstown Wayside Communication Network/5G DCS;
l. Enhancements to the ATS-B Graphical User Interface for improved emergency braking incident diagnostics;
m. Smart Power Distribution Units in ATS-B and Wayside Communication Network Cabinets;
n. New ATS-B command to release switches and overruns;
o. Standardized Local ATS and Line ATS systems for CBTC-centric projects; and
p. Addition of static and dynamic indicators to Human-Machine Interface ATS-B Displays for bridges

2. This Contract scope will also include furnishing new 5G-based carborne DCS equipment that will interface with the existing Siemens carborne CBTC controller equipment on the MTA’s R160 and R179 subway cars. The Contract will also include supporting the installation of the new equipment on the cars as well as integration testing.

##### List and brief description of selection criteria

With regards SPD question 4B.6 SPT will use Dun and Bradstreet's DBAi financial reporting system to assess the financial stability of bidders. Bidders with a Failure Score of 51 or above on the DBAi system will be deemed to have evidenced satisfactory financial stability. Bidders must confirm within their response to the relevant question within the Qualification Envelope
column2: the value of their D&B Failure Score.

column1: In the event that the bidder does not have a Dun and Bradstreet rating or they consider that the rating has been adversely affected by factors which they can offer mitigating evidence for
column2: we will use the following ratios to evaluate a bidder’s financial status. Bidders must confirm within their response to the relevant question within the Qualification Envelope
column3: the name and value of the 3 financial ratios listed below as taken from their own Accounts.

column1: ##### Minimum level(s) of standards possibly required
Profitability – this is taken as profit after tax but before dividends and minority interests. If a company makes a profit then it is a pass for this ratio;
Liquidity – this value is calculated as current assets less stock and work in progress
column2: divided by current liabilities. If the answer is greater than or equal to one then it is a pass for this ratio;

column1: Gearing – this value is calculated as the total external secured borrowing (short term and long term) divided by shareholder funds expressed as a percentage. If the answer is less than or equal to 100% it is considered a pass for this ratio.

column1: ## Conduent Transportation and Flowbird Selected to Equip Paris Ile-de-France Mobilités’ Buses and Trams with Next-Generation Onboard Ticketing Platform

![Conduent Transportation and Flowbird Selected to Equip Paris Ile-de-France Mobilités’ Buses and Trams with Next-Generation Onboard Ticketing Platform](https://cdn.asp.events/CLIENT_CL_Conf_BDA05934_5056_B731_4C9EEBBE0C2416C2/sites/transport-ticketing-globa/media/libraries/TT-content-hub/image-for-paris-flowbird-release.jpeg)
On behalf of its members and the transport authority Ile-de-France Mobilités
column2: Comutitres has selected Conduent Transportation and Flowbird to replace all Ile-de-France bus and tram onboard ticketing platforms with next-generation technology. The Ile-de-France region has a population of approximately 12 million who take about 9 million trips every day on public transport.
which is in charge of ticket management for public (RATP
column3: SNCF) and private (Optile) transport operators on behalf of Ile de France Mobilités
column4: has chosen the Conduent-Flowbird alliance to install a modern onboard ticketing platform to replace outdated equipment across the transit network. In this newly awarded 10-year contract
column5: Conduent and Flowbird will design
column6: install and maintain the driver consoles
column7: ticket validators
column8: central units and communication devices on board the region’s public-transit vehicles.

column1: “The modernization of the Ile-de-France ticketing system is continuing. Ile-de-France Mobilités aims to make life easier for the millions of people using public transport in Ile-de-France thanks to some of the most modern ticketing services in the world. This project is a new stage in our ambitious strategy to transform ticketing
“It is a source of great pride for Comutitres to see
column3: the convergence of the needs and requirements of transport operators in the Paris region with the desire of Ile-de-France Mobilités to implement a common range of ticketing equipment and onboard systems
column5: Managing Director of Comutitres. “Conduent Transportation and Flowbird will help us to implement the desired solution thanks to their experience and expertise in ticketing system modernization projects.”

column1: This new onboard ticketing platform will be one of the largest of its kind based on Information Technology for Public Transport (ITxPT) specifications
column2: which are increasingly becoming an industry standard. The ITxPT association provides specifications and guidelines for open architecture
column3: data and interoperability between IT systems
column4: for public transport and other mobility services.

column2: solutions that encourage modal shift to public transport and optimize the management of urban spaces are at the heart of the needs of city dwellers. The choice of Comutitres to implement open and interoperable technologies fully attests to this
column4: CEO of Flowbird. “With our 600 high-tech engineers in France
column5: specializing in urban mobility
column6: we are pleased to bring our skills and expertise to support Comutitres in the renewal of ticketing in the Ile-de-France region.”

column1: “Comutitres has a bold vision of a modern
column2: technologically advanced system that will equip the buses and trams of the Ile-de-France region and become a model for the world
column5: Public Transit at Conduent Transportation. “Given our more than 50 years of expertise in this area
column6: our team is well positioned to help Comutitres implement an onboard solution at scale for one of the largest transit systems in the world. Ile-de-France transit authorities and operators have been valued clients of Conduent for more than 25 years
column7: and we’re honored to expand our partnership on this major project.”

# Austin Light Rail gets 'medium-high' rating from federal transit officials

column2: Texas — The Austin Light Rail project is creeping closer to the station after a promising rating from federal transit officials last week.

column1: In an [annual funding recommendation report](https://www.transit.dot.gov/sites/fta.dot.gov/files/2025-11/FY26-AR-Appendix-A-Project-Ratings_0.pdf) from the Federal Transit Administration
column2: the project received an overall project rating and local financial commitment summary rating of “medium-high.” It also received “medium” ratings for mobility improvement
column3: land use and project justification summary.

column1: The line received a cost-effectiveness rating of “low
column2: ” which may reflect the problems the Austin Light Rail has faced since its 2020 voter approval as part of Project Connect. The project included “a generational $7 billion transit overhaul and an ongoing 20% property tax increase
column4: but the cost of the light rail has since increased.

column1: The funding recommendation ratings are set to allow the Austin Transit Partnership to compete for federal funding through the Capital Investment Grant. The line’s overall rating also “signals that federal staff view the project as financially and technically viable

Austin Light Rail was 27 miles long at the time of Project Connect’s passing but has since been reduced to under 10 miles. It would run north to south between 38th and Oltorf streets and include an eastern spur stretching from Lady Bird Lake area to near the inside of State Highway 71.

column1: # Singapore’s LTA looks to the future with cloud-based fleet management technology from Trapeze Group

**The world-leading public transport services delivered by Singapore’s Land Transport Authority (LTA) are set to be enhanced by a cloud-based Bus Fleet Management System (BFMS) implemented by the Trapeze Group-ST Engineering consortium.**
The consortium successfully implemented a Centralised Fleet Management System that has supported Singapore’s bus fleet since 2014 and currently oversees about 5

# NTA signs new contract with Go-Ahead Ireland to support roll-out of new bus network in Dublin
**Five-Year contract to deliver an additional 17.5 million kilometres annually as part of the BusConnects Network Redesign**
### **Minister marks NTA – Go-Ahead Ireland five year contract for Outer Dublin Metropolitan Area (ODMA)**

**Minister for Transport Darragh O’Brien** joined the National Transport Authority (NTA) and Go-Ahead Ireland to mark the signing of a significant five-year contract to operate **[Public Service Obligation (PSO) bus services](https://www.transportforireland.ie/getting-around/by-bus/tfi-bus-services/)** across the Outer Dublin Metropolitan Area (ODMA).

[![NTA signs new Go-Ahead Ireland pictured with Minister for Transport Darragh O'Brien](https://www.nationaltransport.ie/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/NTA-signs-new-Go-Ahead-Ireland-pictured-with-Minister-for-Transport-Darragh-OBrien.jpg)](https://www.nationaltransport.ie/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/NTA-signs-new-Go-Ahead-Ireland-pictured-with-Minister-for-Transport-Darragh-OBrien.jpg)

column2: Go-Ahead Ireland will manage 65 PSO bus routes across the ODMA
column3: including nine term-time routes designed to support local families and strengthen community connectivity. Once fully implemented
column4: these services will add approximately 17.5 million kilometres annually to the Transport for Ireland (TFI) network
column5: enhancing public transport connectivity for communities across North and South Dublin.

column1: ### **New Go-Ahead bus routes started on Saturday

column3: Go-Ahead Ireland began operating 25 routes under the new ODMA North and South contract
column4: while continuing to run 15 routes from the original ODMA contract during a transitional period. These 15 routes will be gradually phased out as new services launch under BusConnects. The remaining 40 routes will be introduced in phases
column5: aligned with the rollout of phases 8 to 11 of the BusConnects Network Redesign.

column1: Go-Ahead Ireland was selected earlier this year as the preferred bidder under a competitive public procurement process.

column2: Go-Ahead Ireland has been operating under contract with the NTA in the Outer Dublin Metropolitan Area
column3: facilitating nearly 97 million passenger journeys to work
column5: medical appointments
column6: and beyond. In 2024
column7: customer satisfaction reached 82%
column8: underscoring the operator’s vital role within the TFI network.

column2: **Minister for Transport Darragh O’Brien** said: “ _This agreement represents a significant investment in the future of public transport in Dublin and delivers on commitments set out in the Programme for Government. Expanding services and improving connectivity are central to our climate and mobility goals
column3: ensuring that more people have access to sustainable
column5: and affordable transport options.”_

column1: ### **Our goal (at the NTA) is to make public transport the first choice for more people**

column2: newly appointed **NTA CEO Anne Shaw** said: _“This contract is a key milestone in our **[BusConnects](https://www.transportforireland.ie/network-redesign/)** programme and in our broader vision for public transport in Dublin. By partnering with Go-Ahead Ireland
column3: we are ensuring that passengers benefit from modern
column5: and customer-focused services. Our goal is to make public transport the first choice for more people
column6: reducing congestion and emissions while improving quality of life.”_

column2: Managing Director of Go-Ahead Ireland** commented: “At Go-Ahead Ireland
column3: our priority has always been our passengers. This new contract enables us to expand our services and our network while continuing to provide safe
column5: and accessible transport across the Greater Dublin Area. Since 2018
column6: we have worked closely with the NTA in operating the ODMA contract and supporting the **[BusConnects Network Redesign](https://www.transportforireland.ie/network-redesign/)**
column7: and that experience forms a strong foundation for our next phase of growth. Our colleagues across the entire business are central to this success
column8: and we look forward to building on this strong foundation as we support our growing communities.”

column1: This partnership with Go-Ahead Ireland underscores the NTA’s commitment to delivering sustainable
column3: and customer-focused public transport across the Dublin region.

column1: The cost of each unit to the city has not been finalized

column1: The DOT has also not indicated how much New Yorkers might have to pay to store their bikes. “From experience
column2: Oh believes a “marginal user fee” is beneficial

column1: “One thing I love about our city is how we are always innovating. We’re constantly deploying new tools to make New York smarter

column5: especially those without space to store a bike at home. Thank you to DOT for the hard work that got us here. I look forward to partnering with Tranzito as we bring this groundbreaking initiative to neighborhoods across the city.”

column1: BC Transit is releasing its pathway to a fully electric provincial fleet. The low carbon fleet program aligns with the Province of BC’s CleanBC plan
column2: supports provincial targets for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions
column3: and supports local government climate action goals. Moving to a fully electric bus fleet will provide customers with a cleaner
column5: and more comfortable transportation journey.

column1: British Columbia’s largest and fastest-growing source of carbon pollution is the transportation sector
column3: Minister of Transportation and Infrastructure. “Changing that trend will be a challenge
column4: but it's also a tremendous opportunity. Many of the actions we need to take to reduce carbon emissions - such as encouraging people to choose transit over their personal vehicles and moving to a fully electric fleet - are the exact same measures that will get us out of gridlock and make our neighbourhoods more liveable
column5: now and for future generations.""

column1: “Through our CleanBC climate and economic plan
column2: which is a shared priority with the BC Green Caucus
column3: our government is making public transit clean
column4: affordable and convenient for people by working together with local partners
column5: ” said George Heyman
column6: Minister of Environment and Climate Change Strategy. “BC Transit’s commitment to a fully electric fleet is an important step towards zero-emissions travel in B.C.
column7: and will mean cleaner air
column8: less congestion and a more comfortable experience for people who choose transit.”

column1: The low carbon fleet program focuses on four principles:
- Transitioning vehicles to electric propulsion based on the fleet replacement plan
- Bridging transition to electric with renewable fuels
- Using business cases to guide investment decisions
The plan is to start purchasing electric buses while converting to new and emerging low carbon technologies
column2: such as compressed natural gas (CNG) which enable the use of renewable natural gas.

column3: Province of BC and BC Transit announced the purchase of our first 10 battery heavy duty electric buses for deployment in 2021. BC Transit is also introducing CNG buses and fueling infrastructure to Victoria and the Central Fraser Valley. This includes the addition of 34 medium duty and 68 heavy duty to the current fleet of 128 compressed natural gas buses.

column1: “Transit can have its biggest impact on reducing greenhouse gas emissions by getting people out of their vehicles and onto the bus. However
column2: we also need to transition our infrastructure
column5: ” said Aaron Lamb
column6: Vice President of Asset Management. “We are excited to be working with the Province of BC and local government partners towards creating a more sustainable future.”

column2: BC Transit will be undertaking our due diligence by monitoring and evaluating technology and infrastructure readiness through this process to ensure we are progressing appropriately. These details will inform future business cases for purchasing electric vehicles.

TTC tunnels might soon get a bit brighter — and more distracting.
Toronto’s transit agency is looking into installing advertisements that would run inside the city’s subway tunnels in time for next summer.

A [proposal presented to the TTC board next week](https://www.toronto.ca/legdocs/mmis/2025/ttc/bgrd/backgroundfile-260684.pdf) could give the transit agency the green light to enter into a 15-year contract with Vancouver-based ad agency [Adtrackmedia](https://www.adtrackmedia.com/about).

column2: 360 vertical LED lights were installed into the walls of the subway tunnel that
column3: when viewed from a speeding train
column4: create a video. The only catch: the trains need to run fast enough for the ads to run.

ST. LOUIS — Saint Louis Public Schools has announced a new transportation partner that will start this summer.
It comes with a $30 million price tag but also promises safety and consistency.
The new company is called [Zum](https://www.ridezum.com/) and is used by schools in places like Kansas City and Omaha.
The school district said they will only need this one vendor to meet all of their transportation needs compared to the 18 they use now.
In the latest update at the St. Louis Public School Board meeting
column2: Chief Operations Officer Square Watson said the on-time percentage of getting students to and from school is currently less than 15%.

column1: Watson said the solution to transportation troubles Zum.
“They had a 98 percent on-time performance rate. They also had zero school bus driver shortages

column1: # Solaris to rejuvenate electric buses in service with battery replacements
**Solaris is introducing a new service to keep ageing electric buses of its early customers up and running. Under the label 'Zero-emission vehicle retrofit
column2: ' the Polish manufacturer now offers the replacement of specific components—primarily batteries. The initiative is meant to prevent older electric buses from being decommissioned.**

column1: ![Solaris urbino electric elektrobus electric bus mpk krakau polen mpk krakow poland min](https://www.electrive.com/media/2025/12/solaris-urbino-18-electric-elektrobus-electric-bus-mpk-krakau-polen-mpk-krakow-poland-2025-01-min-1400x933.jpeg)
Solaris says it wants to offer its customers with earlier-generations of electric buses an effective solution to ensure these vehicles remain competitive in the public transport sector. To this end
column2: the company is turning the replacement of degraded batteries and other components into a standardised service offering. Owners of the electric buses thus have the option to retrofit
column3: i.e. the ‘post-warranty replacement of EV components with new ones’
column4: according to the manufacturer. The new service applies not only to battery-electric buses
column5: but also to battery-trolleybuses and
column6: in future
column7: to Solaris’ hydrogen buses.

column1: Solaris has been producing electric buses since 2011
column3: several thousand of its vehicles are now in operation across Europe— ‘some successfully serving passengers for over a decade and covering nearly a million kilometres’
column4: the company noted. Replacing batteries and other components after the warranty period is designed to help operators ‘in maximising the return on their e-mobility investments’.

column1: Solaris is not the only manufacturer addressing how to support early customers whose electric buses are no longer performing as well. On the one hand
column2: battery capacity naturally degrades over time; on the other
column3: rapid technological advancements in recent years have led to significantly improved and more efficient technology platforms in newer-generation electric buses. Daimler Buses has already introduced [comprehensive battery services for the eCitaro](https://www.electrive.com/2025/04/11/daimler-buses-sets-up-comprehensive-battery-services-for-ecitaro/) this spring and will offer upgrades to the latest battery generation for older vehicles from 2026 onwards.

For comparison: the eCitaro has only been in production since 2018. Solaris
column3: began manufacturing electric buses seven years earlier
column4: making the issue even more pressing for the Polish manufacturer. To put this into perspective: today
column5: the solo bus Urbino 12 electric offers a maximum total energy capacity of [over 600 kWh](https://www.electrive.com/2024/03/06/solaris-gives-urbino-12-electric-battery-update-for-a-good-600-kwh/) for ranges exceeding 600 kilometres. Before 2025
column6: the Urbino 12 electric could accommodate a maximum of [520 kWh](https://www.electrive.com/2022/10/13/new-batteries-for-solaris-electric-buses/)
column7: and before 2022
column8: it was limited to [up to 395 kWh](https://www.electrive.com/2019/10/19/new-batteries-and-longest-bus-so-far-from-solaris/). And before that
column9: even less. The manufacturer describes it as a transformation ‘taking place in a relatively short time with an unprecedented evolution of the solution defined’. That can lead ‘to technological obsolescence during the normal vehicle exploitation period’. Solaris now aims to counteract this and extend the service life of its electric buses. However
column10: the company has not disclosed pricing for this service.

column1: A first battery replacement project is already underway. Solaris is performing battery swaps on 20 buses for MPK Krakow—specifically
column2: 17 Urbino 12 electric and three Urbino 18 electric models. Additional retrofit orders from customers in Berlin
column5: and Warsaw are currently being processed. “We are proud to announce the first units retrofitted in MPK Kraków and ready to scale-up this offering in all Solaris certified service points in Europe
column6: ” commented Marco Franza
column7: Chief Services Officer at Solaris Bus & Coach.

column2: customers receive comprehensive support during the component replacement process. According to Solaris

column5: and complete documentation

column8: it is also ensured that other necessary electric components are replaced
column9: the thermal management system is upgraded (or installed)
column10: and the battery management system is updated. Additionally
column11: Solaris ensures that the modified batteries are compatible with the customer’s existing charging infrastructure.

column1: **MARTA WINS $2.6 MILLION GRANT FROM U.S. DOT TO ELECTRIFY BUS FLEET**
_Purchase of Zero-Emission Electric Buses will Reduce Energy Consumption

**ATLANTA**\- MARTA was awarded this week a $2.6 million grant by the U.S. Department of Transportation for the purchase of six zero-emission battery electric buses to replace six diesel models. Removing the 2005 diesel versions from the fleet will reduce MARTA’s emissions by 935 short tons of greenhouse gases and reduce energy consumption by 14.3 terajoules.

The energy saved by the deployment of the zero-emissions buses is equivalent to nearly 250 years of gas for the average American car driver. Zero-emissions vehicles also prevent the release of fine particulate matter – atmospheric pollutants suspended in the air in the form of minute solid particles or liquid droplets that can be hazardous to health and the environment.
“Receiving the Low-No grant will help MARTA put technologically advanced buses with low-to-no emission in densely populated areas with high ridership routes
column2: ” said MARTA General Manager and CEO Jeffrey Parker. “This welcomed first step toward making our bus fleet environmentally friendly will have community-wide impact. We are grateful to the Center for Transportation and the Environment (CTE) for their partnership and support in the application process and as we plan for installation and ongoing support of the fleet.”

column1: CTE is a nonprofit organization focused on the advancement of clean
column3: innovative transportation and energy technologies. Since its inception in 1993
column4: the organization has helped more than 200 companies improve their transportation technologies and fuels to reduce environmental impact.

column1: “We are excited to partner with MARTA on its first zero-emission bus deployment
column3: CTE’s director of Technology Development. “CTE has helped more than 60 transit agencies across the country deploy zero-emission buses
column4: and we are especially proud to be working with our hometown agency in its critical efforts to advance sustainable transportation for the Atlanta region.”

Network Rail is introducing digital signalling
column2: which will see traditional lineside signals removed and replaced with European Train Control Systems (ETCS) technology. The implementation will enable continuous
column3: real-time information to the train driver's cab.

column1: ETCS is an in-cab signalling system that enables trains to run closer together
column2: safely and to travel at their optimal speeds. To implement ETCS
column3: it requires changes to signalling infrastructure and fitment of in-cab technology on locomotives.

column1: NR is intending to procure a framework to enable the design
column4: test and put back into service of ETCS equipment fitted across a range of existing locomotive classes
column5: including electric
column6: diesel and steam across the Charter & Heritage and Freight sectors.

column1: NR intends to hold a virtual online supplier engagement session for registered parties in relation to the procurement of the Locomotive ETCS Fitment Framework (LFF) on the 2nd of October 2025

This event will have a greater level of detailed information provided on our proposed approaches
column2: and we encourage attendance therefore to help shape the requirements and contracting strategy. Our team will be on hand to ask all questions
column3: and all supply chains are encouraged to attend (large or small) to understand how your organisation can become engaged and involved.

column2: Network Rail will invite all participants to provide feedback on key areas of consideration in the development of the strategy and thinking to date
column3: which will be captured via the Market Sounding Questionnaire (MSQ).

column1: The MSQ will be issued to all interested parties by email to their nominated individual that have registered on BRAVO (JAGGER)
Any clarifications or questions in respect of this market engagement should be directed to:

NR therefore request that should anything be considered a USP and IPR to your organisation that this is marked (CONFIDENTIAL & COMMERCIALLY SENSITIVE) on the clarification or email at the very top of the clarification or email
column2: so that NR can consider ensuring protection of the same.

electric buses comprise approximately 8% of the UTA bus fleet. The transition plan target is 50% alternative fuel vehicles in the fleet by 2040: 36% (or nearly 200 vehicles) powered by electric batteries and 14% fueled by compressed natural gas.

column1: The transition plan includes the development of electric charging infrastructure throughout the UTA service network. [This map](https://www.rideuta.com/-/media/Files/About-UTA/Innovative-Mobility/On_Route_Chargers_map.pdf)  shows current
column3: and potential 300 kW overhead pantograph chargers located strategically in the UTA network for on-route charging. Overnight 150 kW chargers are located at regional bus maintenance facilities. UTA schedules sequential hour-long charging periods during the night to mitigate electrical grid impact.

column1: The Low or No Emission Grant Program (49 U.S.C. 5339(c)) and the Grants for Buses and Bus Facilities Competitive Program (5339(b)) administered by the Federal Transit Administration are major funding sources for this transition plan. The plan includes all the information required to apply for these grants. Additional funding for electric buses may come from the Environmental Protection Agency through the Beehive Emissions Reduction Plan.
**Air Quality Monitoring Project With Live Data Feed**

column2: three TRAX lines have been equipped with research-grade monitors that cover the valley
column3: relaying emissions data every 10 seconds. This project was implemented in partnership with [Salt Lake County](https://www.saltlakecounty.gov/health/air-quality/)
column5: and the Utah Department of Environmental Quality. Visit the [project page](https://atmos.utah.edu/air-quality/trax/index.php) for additional information.

column2: air quality monitors were added to some of UTA’s electric buses. As of 2025
column3: 15 electric buses have been equipped with monitors
column5: PM10
column6: and ozone. This project has allowed for more flexible data collection. Additionally
column7: it provides essential information for future air quality improvement efforts and ensures that communities with the worst air quality receive attention. Visit the [live data feed](https://utahaq.chpc.utah.edu/) for more details.

column1: UTA and Rocky Mountain Power (RMP) enjoy a collaborative partnership in pursuit of joint clean energy projects. This partnership focuses on energy efficiency
column5: and research and grants.

column2: UTA participates in RMP’s Blue Sky program by buying Renewable Energy Certificates to support the green energy supply to the grid. UTA also supports public charging infrastructure at transit park and ride locations like 3900 South Wasatch Boulevard.

column1: UTA is part of the steering committee for Utah’s [electrified transportation action plan](https://le.utah.gov/interim/2024/pdf/00003612.pdf). This collaborative effort builds on the research and development expertise of Advancing Sustainability through Powered Infrastructure for Roadway Electrification (ASPIRE) at Utah State University
column2: as well as partnerships with industry and business across the state.

column1: ASPIRE received $2.1 million in ongoing funding through Senate Bill 125 Transportation Infrastructure Amendments
column2: signed into law by Governor Spencer Cox in March 2023. With this new ongoing funding
column3: ASPIRE will facilitate strategic planning and development to guide Utah to an electrified and intelligent transit system.

column1: In connection with this regional effort
column2: UTA and USU researchers led the development of an energy management system for UTA’s intermodal hub at Salt Lake Central Station. The system mitigates [peak load impacts](https://www.rideuta.com/-/media/Images/About-UTA/Innovative-Mobility-2022/Bus_Grid_Impact2025.png) by momentarily shutting off electric bus fast chargers when TRAX vehicles depart from Salt Lake Central Station. The project's final report can be found [here](https://www.rideuta.com/-/media/Files/About-UTA/Innovative-Mobility/323798RMPAtt10Exhibit17A4_29_2022_intermoal_hub_project_final_report.pdf).

column4: evaluates FCEB performance against conventional CNG buses from January 2020 through December 2021.;"# SunLine Transit Agency Fuel Cell Electric Bus Progress Report - Data Period Focus: Jan. 2020 through Dec. 2021

column1: This report presents early results from a deployment of fuel cell electric buses (FCEBs) operated by SunLine Transit Agency in the Coachella Valley area of California. The five FCEBs
column3: feature an electric drive propulsion system powered by a Ballard fuel cell system. The project team is collaborating with the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) and DOE's National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL) to evaluate the buses in revenue service. The goal of this evaluation is to compare the FCEB performance to that of conventional technology and to track progress over time toward meeting the technical targets set by DOE and the Department of Transportation (DOT). The FCEBs were delivered beginning in mid-2019. The data period covers January 2020 through December 2021. NREL collects data on five 2019 model year compressed natural gas (CNG) buses as a baseline comparison at SunLine. These new CNG buses were phased into service beginning in April 2020.

## Bee-Line System Receives Order Of 106 Hybrid-Electric Buses
Westchester County has announced the completion of the 106 hybrid-electric 40-foot bus delivery. The delivery marks an important milestone for the system
column2: with the fleet now 88 percent hybrid or electric. The new buses
column3: Xcelsior® hybrid-electric heavy-duty transit buses built by New Flyer of America Inc.
column4: a leading bus manufacturer in North America
column5: will provide passengers with a quieter and more comfortable ride while improving air quality for Westchester’s over one million residents.

column1: The new bus seats 40 riders and includes amenities like a bike rack
column2: USB charging ports at every seat
column3: and a driver protective shield. Each bus had a cost of $715
column4: 721 and was approximately 70 percent federally funded. In addition to lowering emissions
column5: they will provide the County with significant savings in fuel.

column1: Latimer said: “My administration is committed to reducing pollution and providing a greener future for all residents. The completion of this delivery represents the latest step our County is taking to meet its sustainability goals. Many people rely on the Bee-Line to get them where they need to go and these buses are a safe
Commissioner of the Westchester County Department of Public Works and Transportation Hugh Greechan said: “As buses have aged out of the fleet
column2: we have made it a priority to purchase more energy-efficient ones that are better for the environment. Of the total 325 bus fleet we have only 40 diesel buses remaining and either hybrid or electric buses will replace them within the next two years. We take great pride in being a leader of sustainable transportation.”

column3: NFI Chris Stoddart said: ""For nearly 15 years
column4: New Flyer has been supporting the Bee-Line with our efficient and safe mobility solutions to move the Westchester County residents. New Flyer’s hybrid-electric buses reduce NOx emissions up to 50% and particulate matter levels up to 100%
column5: bridging the gap between traditional diesel engines and zero-emission propulsion. These new Xcelsior buses will provide an improved passenger experience with lower vehicle emissions and will support the Bee-Line in its sustainability journey.""

column2: the County added its first all-electric bus to the system.  There are currently two 40-foot and four 35-foot all-electric buses that operate out of the Valhalla depot. The County has six electric vehicle chargers within the Valhalla facility and plans on expanding charging infrastructure in the coming years to include the central maintenance facility in Yonkers.

column1: The Bee-Line is Westchester County's bus system
column2: serving over 27 million passengers annually with convenient service connecting residents to jobs
column4: shopping and other regional transportation services. It is the second largest transit bus fleet in New York State
column5: operated by the County’s Department of Public Works and Transportation (DPW&T).

column1: Over 65 percent of all Westchester County residents are within walking distance of a Bee-Line bus route
column2: making the bus both close and convenient. The system has over 3
column3: 300 bus stops and almost 60 routes. All Bee-Line buses are accessible
column4: and designed with many accessibility features
column5: including “kneeling” buses
column6: ramps and lifts.

column1: # Skanska builds tram depot in Helsinki
Skanska has signed a contract with City Transport Ltd to build a tram depot in Helsinki
column2: Finland. The contract is worth EUR 252M
column4: which will be included in the Nordic order bookings for the fourth quarter of 2024.

column1: The depot will be built by an alliance consortium where Skanska is the main contractor. The alliance also includes City Transport Ltd

TfL is seeking to procure a new contract for a railway adhesion control software development services. This notice is issued by TfL to notify the market of our intention to carry out market engagement and a potential future tender which is due to commence in March 2026.
The total contract value is unknown at this time we are embark on this market engagement process. This process does not commit TfL or any potential suppliers to any spend.
The specification has yet to be developed
column2: however the findings from this market engagement process will information the final specification.

column1: To transport the high number of passengers required in a modern city constrained by legacy tunnels and platform lengths needs more frequent trains which in turn requires higher acceleration and brake rates. However
column2: to prevent wheel spin under acceleration or wheel slide under braking requires a minimum level of adhesion (friction) at the wheel rail interface.

column1: Adhesion Controllers Condition Assessment Tool (ACCAT) has been employed over the last 20+ years on the Central line and approaching 6 years on the Sub Surface Railway.

column2: the ACCAT processes weather data provided by a 3rd party and several other inputs to predict the available rail adhesion and outputs recommendations in the following three categories:

column1: 1\. The operation of railhead treatment trains
column2: thus improving the level of adhesion available to trains.
thus reducing the adhesion required to stop the train. It also recommends when an imposed brake rate reduction can be removed

column1: TfL is interested in the innovation software development solutions offered by the market for the development of a custom adhesion prediction tool.
- 72210000 - Programming services of packaged software products
This exercise does not form part of any formal procurement process. All responses will be carefully considered but will not bind TfL to any approach to the procurement
column2: nor will responses be treated as conveying any promise or commitment on the part of the respondent.

column1: \- 2) Interested suppliers submit a response to MS form: [https://forms.office.com/e/mxVLrTJM8Y](https://forms.office.com/e/mxVLrTJM8Y)
\- 3) Preliminary market engagement document issued to interested Suppliers
If you have any questions in regards to this preliminary market engagement process please contact the Procurement Manager
TfL reserves the right to conduct further market engagement in the future if required.

column1: ### HART Awarded $32 Million FTA Grant for CNG Buses
We’re proud to announce that HART has been awarded **$32 million** through the FTA’s Low or No Emission and Bus & Bus Facilities Program — **the largest federal grant in HART’s history**
column3: and one of the top awards in the entire nation this funding cycle.

column1: This investment will allow us to:

column2: 000 miles** — well beyond their useful life

column1: - Reduce annual maintenance costs by **over $1 million**
- Cut harmful emissions across Hillsborough County
- Expand technician training and apprenticeship programs to strengthen our workforce

column2: HART is now positioned to become **Florida’s first transit agency with a 100% low-emission CNG fixed-route fleet** — a major milestone in our Fleet Transition Plan and a big step toward cleaner
column3: more reliable mobility for our community.

column1: This isn’t just an investment in buses. It’s an investment in our riders
column3: and the future of transportation in Hillsborough County.

column1: “Today’s announcement represents a major step forward for reliable transit in Hillsborough County. This investment will help us improve service for riders
column4: and continue transitioning our fleet to cleaner
column5: low-emission technology.

column1: We are profoundly grateful to the more than two dozen partners who stood with us—elected leaders
column5: and industry. Their voices made clear that modern
column6: reliable transit is essential to our region’s growth.”

column1: “This grant is a big win for our Tampa Bay neighbors. Reliable
column2: accessible transit is essential to a healthy local economy—helping people get to work
column5: and the grocery store without added stress
column6: ” said Rep. Kathy Castor.“With these new investments to modernize HART’s fleet and expand mobility options
column7: we’re opening the door to more job opportunities
column8: supporting local businesses and ensuring families across Hillsborough County can move around safely and affordably. I’m proud to help secure these federal resources so every neighbor—regardless of zip code—has the freedom to move
column9: connect and thrive in our community.”

column1: “This federal investment is a significant step forward for transit
in Hillsborough County. With the addition of 33 new CNG buses
who depend on these routes every day. I was proud to support HART’s application
and am grateful to Secretary Duffy and Administrator Molinaro for prioritizing
this project. These upgrades will make a real difference for families
Statement from HART Chair/Councilman Luis Viera﻿
“This $32 million grant for HART is a huge leap forward thanks to our federal partners. HART provides such a pivotal lifeline for our working families – our seniors
column3: our veterans and so many others. I am so thankful for the work ethic
column4: tenacity and determination of our Congresswoman Kathy Castor
column5: who always champions transit. This grant allows us to move forward in a manner that is productive and is consistent with sustainability.”

## Recent History of Denver Transit Service Development
- **October 2018** – Denver partners with Transportation Management Agency (TMA)
column3: a private microtransit service provider to launch a pilot of ‘City Shuttle’
column4: a free-to-ride City transit service between downtown and the Cherry Creek area to help service
column5: retail
column6: and hospitality workers with the commutes.

column1: - **January 2019** – Denver Adopts its First Major Transit Plan
column2: ‘Denver Moves: Transit’.  This first of its kind study recommends development of a Citywide frequent transit network as well as to ""conduct pilot programs in partnership with on-demand/ shared-ride service providers to supplement RTD service"" **.**

column1: - **November 2019** – Denver residents vote to create a City Department of Transportation & Infrastructure (DOTI)
column2: which will act as a modern agency focused on increasing mobility and safety while reducing congestion and fighting climate change.

column1: - **September 2020** – The City and County of Denver kicks off a planning study to lead to a community based pilot service as a follow up to the City Shuttle.
- **October 2021** – The City and County of Denver launches pilot ride-share service that Montbello residents can use to commute to local neighborhood destinations.
- **September 2022** – The City and County of Denver extends pilot in Montbello for two years and expands service to include the  Gateway neighborhood. As part of extension
column2: DOTI also planned the Globeville & Elyria-Swansea (GES) service area. The GES service is expected to launch late Fall

column1: ![City Shuttle powered by Denver the Mile High City logo](https://www.denvergov.org/files/assets/public/doti/images/programsservices/micromobility/transit/city-shuttle-logo.jpg)

column2: the City and County of Denver initiated a planning study to discover where a neighborhood transit service should be operated to serve community needs and improve mobility for its residents. Following a thorough review backed by community engagement and in coordination with RTD
column3: Denver chose Montbello as the first neighborhood to pilot this service.

column2: we led several meetings with stakeholders as well as RTD to better understand what was important to the community when it comes to public transportation. What we learned is that a ride-share program could fill transportation needs of youth
column4: and people who don’t own vehicles in this community.

column1: This pilot originally was intended to run for 12 months and
column3: has now been extended for another two years and was expanded to serve additional neighborhoods.  This pilot will help inform DOTI's future efforts to provide local transit services to Denver's residents and visitors. This ride-share service aims to make it easier for residents to reach neighborhood destinations and connect to regional transit services provided by RTD.

column2: technology-enabled transit service that typically uses multi-passenger/pooled shuttles or vans to provide on-demand or fixed-schedule services with either dynamic or fixed routing.""

column1: This means that microtransit vehicles are often smaller than full sized buses
column2: use an app-based or call-in reservation system for on-demand trips and often serve a smaller geographic area vs. regional fixed-route transit.
